8.5 hours of sleep for an early riser; Lucas is ready for another 6:30am wake-up on Tuesday.

diary: Woke up early as always, got my routine down to a science after thirty years driving. The morning run went smooth—didn't even notice the Light Rail was down between Westlake and University, since I stick to the surface streets anyway. Around mid-morning, I figured why not catch the USA-Wales match? Headed down to The Crocodile with some of the other drivers. Place was packed, everyone in a good mood. The sun was perfect today, made the whole thing feel celebratory. Grabbed a burger and a beer, got caught up in the energy of it. After, I swung by Whole Foods in Capitol Hill for some salmon and vegetables—wanted to cook something decent tonight. Back home by early afternoon, made a nice dinner on the porch. Neighborhood looked beautiful in that golden light. Just sat there for a while, letting the day settle.
thought_process: I almost stayed at the depot for lunch like usual, but the weather and the buzz around the World Cup match got to me. Fifty-four isn't too old to be spontaneous. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect my day since I drive my own car—just made me realize how dependent some folks are on that system. Took a longer route home through Capitol Hill instead of straight back to Ballard, gave myself an excuse to grab better groceries. My mood shifted from routine to something lighter, more present. The match didn't matter much to me personally, but being around other people enjoying themselves, it reminded me why I like living in a city. The porch time sealed it—just what I needed after a day of other people's schedules.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
